page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs derived from 10 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

This server is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated GPUs, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the E20s_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, with gzip compression reaching 35.43 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.68 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registers at 132.85 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is measured at 36.55 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1518.31 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds reach 139.09 GB/sec and 57.2 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v7 provides a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high L3 cache capacity, and 160.0 GB of RAM makes this instance suitable for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application hosting, and data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Esv7 family, powered by a 10-core Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It provides 20 dedicated vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 480 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage. In benchmark evaluations, the E20s_v7 achieves top-tier results in single-threaded operations, with gzip compression at 35.43 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.68 GB/sec. It delivers average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, with large-block uncached reads at 139.09 GB/sec. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, enterprise applications, and database hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, suitable for database hosting and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and high-density memory configurations for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 10-core Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-threaded performance with 35.43 GB/sec gzip compression
No bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external storage solutions
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector operations
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
